display.purpose: Page title only. 'Morning Hoops' with italic accent.

headline.purpose: Section titles per tab view. Instrument Serif.

title.purpose: Stat numbers, ranking numerals, 7/7 Club names. Instrument Serif numbers.

body.purpose: Commentary, descriptions, editorial notes. Outfit. Max 600px width.

label.purpose: Section headers, tier badges, club designations. Outfit uppercase.

components.name: Team Badge (Blue Win)
components.description: Compact result indicator for Blue team victories. Appears at the end of each game row.
components.css: .ds-badge-blue { font-size: 9px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 3px 8px; border-radius: 4px; background: rgba(91,141,239,.15); color: #5B8DEF; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; display: inline-block; }

components.name: Team Badge (White Win)
components.description: Compact result indicator for White team victories.
components.css: .ds-badge-white { font-size: 9px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 3px 8px; border-radius: 4px; background: rgba(180,188,208,.1); color: #B4BCD0; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; display: inline-block; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Primary container for all content sections. Flat, bordered, rounded.
components.html:
TOP 5 WIN RATES
Card content area
components.css: .ds-card { background: #16161A; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06); border-radius: 14px; padding: 20px; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; color: #EDEDEF; } .ds-card-label { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 1.6px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #52525B; margin-bottom: 14px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 13px; color: #A1A1AA; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Tab Navigation
components.description: Horizontal tab bar for switching between Summary, Season, and monthly views.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; gap: 2px; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06); } .ds-tab { background: none; border: none; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; padding: 10px 16px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; color: #52525B; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; transition: all 0.15s; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-tab:hover { color: #A1A1AA; } .ds-tab-active { font-weight: 700; color: #EDEDEF; border-bottom-color: #EF6234; } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EF6234; outline-offset: -2px; }

components.name: Attendance Tier Badge
components.description: Tiny classification chip showing player attendance tier (IRON, REG, PT, DROP, 1x).
components.css: .ds-tier { font-size: 8px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 1px; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 3px; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; display: inline-block; } .ds-tier-iron { background: rgba(239,98,52,0.09); color: #EF6234; } .ds-tier-reg { background: rgba(52,211,153,0.09); color: #34D399; } .ds-tier-pt { background: rgba(91,141,239,0.09); color: #5B8DEF; } .ds-tier-drop { background: rgba(251,191,36,0.09); color: #FBBF24; }

components.name: Theme Toggle
components.description: Dark/light mode switch in the top-right corner of the header.
components.css: .ds-theme-toggle { background: #16161A; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06); border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 14px; cursor: pointer; color: #A1A1AA; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; transition: background 0.2s; } .ds-theme-toggle:hover { background: #1C1C20; } .ds-theme-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #EF6234; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Progress Bar
components.description: Horizontal fill bar for win-rate visualization. Color follows the Three-Tier Stat Rule.
components.css: .ds-progress { height: 6px; background: #0D0D0F; border-radius: 3px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-progress-fill { height: 100%; border-radius: 3px; } .ds-progress-green { background: #34D399; } .ds-progress-gold { background: #FBBF24; } .ds-progress-red { background: #F87171; }

components.name: Week Header
components.description: Non-data separator row inserted before each week's games in month tables. Inset background with label typography showing 'WEEK N · date–date' on the left and a Blue/White win tally on the right. Follows the inset-panel-within-card pattern used by attendance table headers and Debrief footer.
components.html: WEEK 1 · Mar 3–Mar 7Blue 3 White 2
components.css: .ds-week-header { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; background: #0D0D0F; border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.06); padding: 8px 16px; font-family: 'Outfit', sans-serif; } .ds-week-header-label { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 1.6px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #52525B; } .ds-week-header-tally { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 1.6px; text-transform: uppercase; display: flex; gap: 10px; } .ds-week-header-blue { color: #5B8DEF; } .ds-week-header-white { color: #B4BCD0; }

narrative.overview:
Morning Hoops is a sports dashboard that operates like a small-circulation newspaper written by and for a group of fifteen adults who play pickup basketball at 4:45 AM. The design system serves two masters simultaneously: dense statistical data (win-loss records, partnership matrices, attendance tracking) and a relentless editorial voice that treats a middle school gym like Madison Square Garden. The result is a product that looks like a stats dashboard but reads like a humor column.

The visual system is intentionally restrained so the writing can be loud. Dark surfaces, tight typography, and flat tonal layering create a stage; the orange-ember accent and the Instrument Serif display face provide just enough character to feel authored rather than generated. The system rejects anything that looks like a fantasy sports app, a corporate analytics tool, or a styled spreadsheet. It also rejects anything that takes itself too seriously.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-first: designed for phones in a dim gym at 5 AM
Flat and tonal: depth through background tinting, never shadows
Serif + sans pairing: editorial headlines over functional body text
Single accent color at high saturation against near-black surfaces
Dense but scannable: lots of data, no wasted space, every section earns its scroll
Editorial commentary woven into every data section, not siloed

rules.name: The Ember Rule
rules.body: Ember Orange is the only warm hue in the system. It marks editorial voice: section labels, commentary callouts, the title italic, tab indicators, and the 'Debrief' tag. If Ember is used on a non-editorial element, the system is drifting.

rules.name: The Three-Tier Stat Rule
rules.body: Player performance is always color-coded: Green (≥60%), Gold (45-59%), Red (<45%). These three colors are functional, not decorative. They never appear outside of stat contexts.

rules.name: The Italic-Is-Ember Rule
rules.body: Instrument Serif italic appears exactly twice: in the page title ('Hoops' set in accent color) and in the 'vs' separator between teams. Italic is never used for emphasis in body text.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are prohibited. If a new component 'needs' a shadow, it needs a redesign. Use the tonal stack or a border.

dos: Do use Ember Orange exclusively for editorial voice elements: labels, commentary markers, the title accent, tab indicators.
Do maintain the three-tier tonal stack (void → card → inset) for all depth.
Do pair every stat with context. A number without commentary is a missed opportunity.
Do use Instrument Serif for display numbers, headlines, and the 'vs' separator.
Do keep editorial notes in 10px Ember italic below game rows.
Do test every new element in dark mode first.
Do respect mobile: content padded at 20px sides, tables use overflow-x auto.
donts: Don't add shadows or box-shadow to any element.
Don't use Ember Orange for non-editorial elements (buttons, backgrounds, structural borders).
Don't make it look like a fantasy sports app. No ESPN card layouts, no player headshots in circles.
Don't make it look like a corporate SaaS dashboard. No 'metrics that matter' energy.
Don't make it look like a styled Google Sheet.
Don't take the design too seriously. If a component looks like it belongs in Datadog, it doesn't belong here.
Don't use Inter, Roboto, Arial, or system-ui.
